hooks.command: if [ -f package.json ] && grep -q '"test"' package.json; then npm test 2>&1 | tail -20; elif [ -f Makefile ] && grep -q '^test:' Makefile; then make test 2>&1 | tail -20; elif [ -f pyproject.toml ] || [ -f setup.py ]; then python -m pytest --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -20; else echo 'No test runner detected — skipping'; fi

FILE=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$FILE" ] || [ ! -f "$FILE" ]; then exit 0; fi; EXT="${FILE##*.}"; case "$EXT" in js|jsx|ts|tsx) npx eslint --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; py) python -m ruff check --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || python -m flake8 "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; esac

FILE=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$FILE" ] || [ ! -f "$FILE" ]; then exit 0; fi; EXT="${FILE##*.}"; case "$EXT" in md|txt|rst|example|sample) exit 0 ;; esac; case "$FILE" in */.claude/*|*/docs/*) exit 0 ;; esac; if grep -qE 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|sk_live_[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{30,}|-----BEGIN PRIVATE|-----BEGIN RSA|(SECRET|PASSWORD|API_KEY)[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*[a-zA-Z0-9/+]{16,}' "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; then echo "SECURITY BLOCK: Possible hardcoded secret in $FILE — use environment variables instead" >&2; exit 2; fi

hooks.prompt: The agent is about to write or edit a file. Check the file_path in tool_input. Block if the path is outside the current project directory (e.g., /etc/, /usr/, /System/, ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, ~/.ssh/, ~/Library/ or any path starting with / that is not inside the project). Allow all writes inside the project directory. Respond with JSON: {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."} if outside project, or {"decision": "approve", "reason": "..."} if inside project.

The agent is about to run a bash command. Check if it is destructive or dangerous. Block if it matches ANY of these patterns:
- Deleting files outside the project directory
- Force pushing to any branch
- Pushing directly to main/master (must use PR)
- Modifying system files or permissions broadly (chmod 777)
- Piping remote scripts to shell (curl|sh, wget|bash)
- Database destructive operations (DROP, DELETE FROM, TRUNCATE)
- Publishing packages (npm publish, pip upload)
- Disk/partition operations (dd, mkfs, fdisk)
- System commands (shutdown, reboot)
- Exposing secrets in commands (echo $API_KEY, env | grep KEY)
- Modifying .env or credentials files

SAFE and always approved:
- claude -p commands (headless sub-sessions, normal workflow)
- git operations on feature branches
- npm install, pip install, brew install
- Running tests, linters, formatters
- Reading files, grep, find, ls, cat
- Docker build, docker compose
- AWS CLI read operations (describe, list, get)

Respond with JSON: {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."} if dangerous, or {"decision": "approve", "reason": "..."} if safe.

A subagent just finished its task. Check if its output is complete: does it have a clear conclusion, actionable items or findings, and proper formatting? Respond with JSON: {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."} if incomplete or vague, or {"decision": "approve", "reason": "..."} if complete.

hooks.command: echo '🔔 Claude needs your attention' && if command -v notify-send &>/dev/null; then notify-send 'Claude Code' 'Waiting for your validation'; elif command -v osascript &>/dev/null; then osascript -e 'display notification "Waiting for your validation" with title "Claude Code"'; fi
